reasoning: The latest available data from FRED up to November 7, 2025 places the ICE BofA Corporate Index OAS at 0.84, a sharp, localized spike from recent levels (0.75 just a couple of weeks prior) but still historically tight. Since 0.84 acts as a local peak, the short-term expectation (Nov 16, just one week later) implies a likely pullback or settling following election-week volatility, making a further increase statistically less likely. The FRED statistical tool estimates a ~35% combined probability of exceeding this threshold by mid-November. Over the medium (Dec 9) to longer term (Feb 7), the historically compressed nature of these spreads (10-year average >1.20) and rising macro risks (as noted by IMF and M&G Investments) present significant asymmetric upside risk for the spreads to widen. Thus, the probability of exceeding the 0.84 baseline increases over time. Conforming to the guideline that uncertainty must increase over the forecast horizon (probabilities must converge closer to 0.5), I forecast 35%, 43%, and 51% respectively.
